title: Inmofinder
description: InmoFinder is an AI-powered real estate aggregation platform built by a team of four developers. It centralizes property listings from multiple websites into a single platform using web scraping to extract structured data, including property features, pricing, and images, making home searching faster and more efficient.
longDescription:
InmoFinder is a collaborative project developed by a team of four engineering students with the goal of simplifying the real estate search experience in Medellín. Instead of browsing multiple property websites, users can access a centralized platform that aggregates listings from different sources, providing a faster and more convenient way to discover available homes.

One of the project's core innovations was the integration of AI-assisted web scraping to collect and structure real estate data from multiple websites. The system automatically extracts valuable information such as price, location, number of bedrooms, bathrooms, parking spaces, property descriptions, and images, transforming unstructured web content into consistent, searchable data.

Beyond building the platform itself, the project required designing a scalable data collection workflow, handling information from different sources, and ensuring that property details remained organized and accessible. Working as part of a multidisciplinary team also strengthened our collaboration, software architecture, and problem-solving skills while delivering a solution that demonstrates how AI can enhance data acquisition and improve user experience in the real estate industry.

title: MCDM Project
description: Engineered a robust Multi-Criteria Decision-Making (MCDM) platform to identify the optimal location for a wind farm in Colombia by evaluating multiple criteria, including wind potential,  infrastructure, and armed conflict. The project combines expert judgment with algorithm-driven weighting methods to deliver data-driven decision support for complex scenarios.
longDescription:
This project was developed by a team of five as part of a larger research initiative involving university professors and an industry partner. Our goal was to contribute a decision-support algorithm capable of identifying the most suitable locations for wind farm development in Colombia by considering multiple technical, environmental, social, and economic criteria.

Throughout the project, we faced several engineering challenges beyond the decision-making model itself. One of the first was finding an efficient spatial representation of the country, which led us to adopt the H3 hexagonal indexing system. As the dataset grew, memory consumption became a significant bottleneck, so we redesigned the processing pipeline to execute computations in chunks, allowing the algorithm to scale without exhausting system resources.

After reviewing and comparing multiple research papers on Multi-Criteria Decision-Making (MCDM), we designed a hybrid methodology combining the Best-Worst Method (BWM) to determine criteria weights from both expert knowledge and algorithmic analysis, with the PROMETHEE ranking method to evaluate every H3 cell across Colombia. This approach successfully highlighted the most promising areas while reducing visual noise from less suitable alternatives, making the results easier to interpret and analyze.

Due to confidentiality agreements, the repository uses a synthetic dataset instead of the real project data. However, the sample data was carefully generated to reflect realistic geographic and territorial conditions, allowing the methodology and software architecture to be demonstrated without exposing sensitive information.

Beyond implementing the algorithms, this project strengthened my experience in geospatial computing, optimization, scientific research, and collaborative software development, while demonstrating how data-driven decision-making can support complex real-world infrastructure planning.

title: Nextstep
description: NextStep is a gamified career platform developed by a team of four for a Magneto Empleos innovation challenge. The solution increases user engagement and profile completion through achievements, rewards, personalized notifications, and an AI-powered career assistant that supports users throughout their job search journey.
longDescription:
NextStep was developed by a team of four engineering students as a solution to a real-world innovation challenge proposed by Magneto Empleos. The objective was to increase user Lifetime Value (LTV) by encouraging job seekers to engage more actively with the platform and complete their professional profiles, enabling more accurate candidate-to-company matching.

Our approach combined gamification principles with user-centered design to transform profile completion into a rewarding experience. We implemented an achievement system with badges, progress tracking, and personalized notifications that motivated users to remain active and continuously improve their profiles instead of interacting with the platform only when searching for jobs.

To further enhance the experience, we developed Jobbie, an AI-powered career assistant designed to guide users throughout their employability journey. From answering career-related questions to encouraging profile optimization and platform engagement, Jobbie complemented the gamification strategy by providing personalized support. The project demonstrated how combining behavioral design, AI, and modern web technologies can improve user retention while creating greater value for both job seekers and recruiters.
